ground_truth.title: Entry Level Accountant Academy (7 CPE Credits)
ground_truth.author: Bill Hanna
ground_truth.main_content: Entry-Level Accountant Academy - Learn How To Perform The Essential Tasks is a practical online course for anyone starting a career in accounting. In just 4.5 hours of video lessons across 3 modules, you'll learn to apply the Accounting equation, record revenues and expenses under US GAAP, and make end-of-period adjustments with confidence. You'll also master the Accounts Payable process—from invoice approval to payments—and get hands-on with QuickBooks and automation tools to streamline workflows. By the end of the course, you'll be equipped with real-world, job-ready accounting skills that employers value—perfect for students, new hires, or career changers. After completing this course, the learner should be able to: Define the Accounting Equation and apply it to the various types of business transactions; Apply US GAAP principles to revenues and expenses recording; Identify revenue and costs adjustments at the end of the financial period; Distinguish between all tasks in Accounts Payable – including vendor invoice recording, approval, and payment processes in an accounts payable system; Apply Accounting rules in the implementation of systems (QuickBooks) to streamline the financial processes. Course Details: Duration: 4.5 hours; Access Duration: Lifetime; Field of Study: Accounting; CPE Credits - NASBA: 7; Knowledge Level: Basic; Delivery Method: QAS Self-Study (on-demand video lectures); Prerequisites: High school degree or equivalent; Advanced Prep: None. Course includes: 4.5 hours of bit-size video lectures by Bill Hanna; Excel Templates to practice what you see in the lectures; 25+ Quizzes to practice your learning along the way; 7 CPE Credits of completion when you finish the course, which is a downloadable PDF you can share on Linkedin; 14 days Money back guarantee if you are not 100% satisfied! Curriculum: Module 1 - The Basics of Accounting (35 Minutes, 9 lectures, 3 quizzes): Covers the basics of accounting, double-entry system, debits and credits using DEALER and equation methods, the flow of accounting data, creation of financial statements, and ethics in accounting; Module 2 - Recording Revenue & Expense Transactions (2 hours, 20 lectures, 6 Excel templates, 3 quizzes): Focuses on practical setup and recording of transactions—billing, invoicing, payroll, vendor payments, and expense tracking—with real-world tools and accounting impact for each step; Module 3 - End of Period Adjustments (1 hour, 15 lectures, 3 Excel templates, 3 quizzes): Covers the adjusting entries process for accruals, deferrals, and estimates, bank reconciliation, and the creation of financial statements.

I have had 18+ years of progressive roles in Accounting and Finance, both in Manufacturing and SAAS.
